##### II.2.a.b — AI does not expect: the mistake

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META:** Most contestable pin. The node is competent pursuit of a misspecified goal, not betrayal. Christiano placed by tractability + evidence, and he is the one genuine straddle (his "bang" edges to the leaf above); Amodei placed by mechanism, his race-logic decision-context. *Sleeper Agents* is strictly deception, so it backs the broad "methods are unreliable" claim, not this node specifically.
:::

Here the system understands catastrophe in the abstract, but its own chosen action does not flag as one — not a betrayal, but competent pursuit of a misspecified goal, and we lose control without anyone intending it. The risk is high yet less brittle than a treacherous turn: the likely reality is simply that institutions cannot reliably align advanced capability before they deploy it. Anthropic's *Sleeper Agents* showed [standard safety training failing to remove hidden misbehavior](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.05566). Paul Christiano puts [irreversible takeover near 22%](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/xWMqsvHapP3nwdSW8/my-views-on-doom) and Dario Amodei [around 25%](https://www.axios.com/2025/09/17/anthropic-dario-amodei-p-doom-25-percent); both update on evidence and expect a path-dependent, partly-catchable loss of control rather than a single locked door. Christiano is the one genuine straddle here, his sharper scenarios edging toward the knowing optimizer above, while Amodei's lab-building is a bet about the race, not a second threat model. Deliberate misuse of an aligned system by a bad actor sits awkwardly across this tree, since there the intent is human, not the machine's.
